claim_boundary.actual: evidence_commit identifies the commit that last changed the cryptographic publication evidence; truth_subject_commit identifies the transparency-verified subject commit. Consumers must not equate them unless evidence_equals_subject is true.
claim_boundary.expected: evidence_commit identifies the commit that last changed the cryptographic publication evidence; truth_subject_commit identifies the transparency-verified subject commit. Consumers must not equate them unless evidence_equals_subject is true.

head_claim_boundary.actual: Current HEAD contains transparency-verified evidence for truth_subject_commit; HEAD is not itself the verified subject.
head_claim_boundary.expected: Current HEAD contains transparency-verified evidence for truth_subject_commit; HEAD is not itself the verified subject.

platform_governance_claim_boundary.actual: Current-head workflow governance is fresh, but GitHub branch-protection / ruleset enforcement is not proven on main. Do not claim platform-enforced governance while main_branch_protection_receipt is not PASS.
platform_governance_claim_boundary.expected: Current-head workflow governance is fresh, but GitHub branch-protection / ruleset enforcement is not proven on main. Do not claim platform-enforced governance while main_branch_protection_receipt is not PASS.

platform_governance_head_claim_boundary.actual: Current HEAD contains workflow-governance-only evidence for platform_governance_subject_commit; it is not itself freshly governance-proven.
platform_governance_head_claim_boundary.expected: Current HEAD contains workflow-governance-only evidence for platform_governance_subject_commit; it is not itself freshly governance-proven.

stronger_claim_not_made: WS20 proves only same-host independent clean-environment verification of the sealed detached verifier package. It does not claim cross-host or third-party reproduction, full artifact reconstruction beyond attested-subject verification, public horizon opening, or any WS21 public-release claim.

verification_target.publication_surface_boundary: For WS18, publication surface means the sealed WS15 canonical delivery publication bundle carried forward by the WS16 critical artifact set; it does not collapse that artifact set into the earlier WS7 authority-subject publication surface.
